modelIdentification.name: SISO-STD-001.1-2025 - Real-time Platform Reference Distributed Emission Regeneration FOM Module
modelIdentification.purpose: The RPR FOM supports interoperability for real-time, platform oriented defense simulation.
modelIdentification.description: Represent electromagnetic emissions using the regeneration principle.

reference.identification: SISO-STD-001.1-2025 - Real-time Platform Reference Foundation FOM Module
SISO-STD-001.1-2025 - Real-time Platform Reference Enumerations FOM Module
SISO-STD-001.1-2025 - Real-time Platform Reference Base FOM Module
Standard for Guidance, Rationale, and Interoperability Modalities for the Real-time Platform Reference Federation Object Model (RPR FOM) SISO-STD-001-2025 4 November 2025
IEEE Standard for Distributed Interactive Simulation - Application Protocols IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 19 December 2012

objectClass.name: Designator
objectClass.semantics: A system used to designate or mark a location or object, such as a laser designator which supports a laser-guided weapon engagement.

attribute.name: CodeName
attribute.semantics: The code name of the designator system.

attribute.name: DesignatedObjectIdentifier
attribute.semantics: The object instance ID of the entity that is currently being designated (if any).

attribute.name: DesignatorCode
attribute.semantics: The designator code being used by the designating entity.

attribute.name: DesignatorEmissionWavelength
attribute.semantics: The wavelength of the designator system.

attribute.name: DesignatorOutputPower
attribute.semantics: The output power of the designator system.

attribute.name: DesignatorSpotLocation
attribute.semantics: The location, in the world coordinate system, of the designator spot.

attribute.name: DeadReckoningAlgorithm
attribute.semantics: Dead reckoning algorithm used by the issuing object.

attribute.name: RelativeSpotLocation
attribute.semantics: The location of the designator spot, relative to the object being designated (if any).

attribute.name: SpotLinearAccelerationVector
attribute.semantics: The rate of change in linear velocity of the designator spot over time.

objectClass.name: EmitterSystem
objectClass.semantics: A device that radiates an electromagnetic signal, such as a radar or jammer.

attribute.name: EmitterFunctionCode
attribute.semantics: The function of the emitter system.

attribute.name: EmitterType
attribute.semantics: The type of the emitter system.

attribute.name: EmitterIndex
attribute.semantics: A unique number, which uniquely identifies the emitter system from other on the same host entity.

attribute.name: EventIdentifier
attribute.semantics: The EventIdentifier is used by the generating federate to associate related events. The event number shall start at one at the beginning of the exercise, and be incremented by one for each event.

objectClass.name: IFFSystem
objectClass.semantics: Cooperative or noncooperative military IFF transponder and interrogator systems, including military Battlefield Target Identification Device (BTID) systems used on military vehicles, and cooperative civilian Air Traffic Control (ATC) radar beacon systems.

attribute.name: SystemType
attribute.semantics: General type of IFF/SSR system in use.

attribute.name: SystemName
attribute.semantics: Particular named type of the IFF/SSR system in use.

attribute.name: SystemDesignator
attribute.semantics: A unique decimal number assigned to this interrogator or transponder to distinguish it from multiple interrogators or transponders that are associated with the same entity.

attribute.name: InteractiveModeIsActive
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether or not the IFFSystem is currently operating in the Interactive Mode.

attribute.name: EventIdentifier
attribute.semantics: Used to associate related events.

attribute.name: SystemIsOn
attribute.semantics: Whether or not the system is on.

attribute.name: SystemMode
attribute.semantics: Mode of operation.

attribute.name: OperationalStatus
attribute.semantics: Whether the system is operational or whether it has experienced a system failure.

attribute.name: TestModeIsOn
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether the IFF/SSR system has been placed in the equipment test mode.

attribute.name: EmissionsDataAvailable
attribute.semantics: Specifies if emissions data (DIS IFF PDU layer 2) is available for this IFF/SSR system. When set to True, then the EmissionsDataSpecificInformation, BeamAzimuthCenter, BeamAzimuthSweep, BeamElevationCenter, BeamElevationSweep, BeamSweepSync, and FundamentalParameterData attributes shall be generated.

attribute.name: EmissionsDataSpecificInformation
attribute.semantics: Layer-specific information for emissions data (layer number 2) that varies by SystemType.

attribute.name: BeamAzimuthCenter
attribute.semantics: The azimuth center of the IFF/SSR beam's scan volume relative to the IFF/SSR system.

attribute.name: BeamAzimuthSweep
attribute.semantics: The azimuth half-angle of the IFF/SSR beam's scan volume relative to the IFF/SSR system.

attribute.name: BeamElevationCenter
attribute.semantics: The elevation center of the IFF/SSR beam's scan volume relative to the IFF/SSR system.

attribute.name: BeamElevationSweep
attribute.semantics: The elevation half-angle of the IFF/SSR beam's scan volume relative to the IFF/SSR system.

attribute.name: BeamSweepSync
attribute.semantics: The percentage of time a scan is through its pattern from its origin.

attribute.name: FundamentalParameterData
attribute.semantics: The fundamental energy radiation characteristics of the IFF/SSR system.

attribute.name: AntennaLocationArray
attribute.semantics: The location of each auxiliary IFF/SSR antenna. An auxiliary IFF/SSR antenna is one that is in addition to the primary antenna whose location is contained in the EmbeddedSystem attribute RelativePosition.

attribute.name: DataCommunicationsSpecificInformation
attribute.semantics: Layer-specific information for data communications (layer number 5) that varies by SystemType.

objectClass.name: Interrogator
objectClass.semantics: A transmitter that emits a signal to trigger a response from an IFF/SSR system.

objectClass.name: Transponder
objectClass.semantics: A system that produces IFF/SSR responses when it receives an appropriate signal.

attribute.name: EmergencyOn
attribute.semantics: Set to True when the transponder has set Military Emergency to On.

attribute.name: IdentSquawkFlashOn
attribute.semantics: Set to True when the transponder has set the I/P to On. This is also referred to as the Special Process Indicator (SPI), "Squawk Flash" or "Squawk Ident".

attribute.name: UnmannedAircraftOn
attribute.semantics: Set to True when the transponder has set the Unmanned Aircraft reply to On.

objectClass.name: TCAS
objectClass.semantics: TCAS or ACAS transceiver system.

objectClass.name: EmitterBeam
objectClass.semantics: A sector of concentrated energy from a device that radiates an electromagnetic signal.

attribute.name: BeamAzimuthCenter
attribute.semantics: The azimuth center of the emitter beam's scan volume relative to the emitter system.

attribute.name: BeamAzimuthSweep
attribute.semantics: The azimuth half-angle of the emitter beam's scan volume relative to the emitter system.

attribute.name: BeamElevationCenter
attribute.semantics: The elevation center of the emitter beam's scan volume relative to the emitter system.

attribute.name: BeamElevationSweep
attribute.semantics: The elevation half-angle of the emitter beam's scan volume relative to the emitter system.

attribute.name: BeamFunctionCode
attribute.semantics: The function of the emitter beam.

attribute.name: BeamIdentifier
attribute.semantics: The identification of the emitter beam (must be unique on the emitter system).

attribute.name: BeamParameterIndex
attribute.semantics: The index, into the federation specific emissions database, of the current operating mode of the emitter beam.

attribute.name: EffectiveRadiatedPower
attribute.semantics: The effective radiated power for the emission (in the direction of the strongest beam). For a radar or a noise jammer, this attribute shall represent the peak ERP.

attribute.name: EmissionFrequency
attribute.semantics: The center frequency of the emitter beam.

attribute.name: EmitterSystemIdentifier
attribute.semantics: The identification of the emitter system that is generating this emitter beam.

attribute.name: EventIdentifier
attribute.semantics: The EventIdentifier is used by the generating federate to associate related events. The event number shall start at one at the beginning of the exercise, and be incremented by one for each event.

attribute.name: FrequencyRange
attribute.semantics: The bandwidth of the frequencies covered by the emitter beam.

attribute.name: PulseRepetitionFrequency
attribute.semantics: The Pulse Repetition Frequency of the emitter beam.

attribute.name: PulseWidth
attribute.semantics: The pulse width of the emitter beam.

attribute.name: SweepSynch
attribute.semantics: The percentage of time a scan is through its pattern from its origin.

objectClass.name: RadarBeam
objectClass.semantics: A sector of concentrated energy from a device that radiates an electromagnetic signal.

attribute.name: HighDensityTrack
attribute.semantics: When set to Selected (1), the receiving simulation can assume that all targets that are in the scan pattern of the radar beam are being tracked.

attribute.name: TrackObjectIdentifiers
attribute.semantics: Identification of the entities being tracked.

attribute.name: BlankingSectors
attribute.semantics: Information about the radar beam's blanking sectors.

objectClass.name: JammerBeam
objectClass.semantics: An emitter beam that is designed to jam or otherwise interfere or confuse another emitter system.

attribute.name: JammingModeSequence
attribute.semantics: The jamming mode technique or series of techniques being applied.

attribute.name: JammedObjectIdentifiers
attribute.semantics: Identification of the emitter beams being jammed.

attribute.name: HighDensityJam
attribute.semantics: When set to Selected (1), the receiving simulation can assume that all emitter beams that are in the scan pattern of the jammer beam are being jammed.

attribute.name: AngleDeceptionData
attribute.semantics: Information about angle deception jamming.

attribute.name: FalseTargetsData
attribute.semantics: Information about false targets jamming.

objectClass.name: IFFData
objectClass.semantics: Data applicable to distinct IFF/SSR system capabilities.

attribute.name: IFFSystemIdentifier
attribute.semantics: The identification of the IFFSystem object to which the IFF/SSR capability is composed.

attribute.name: EventIdentifier
attribute.semantics: Used to associate related events.

attribute.name: ReportingSimulation
attribute.semantics: The simulation publishing this IFF data.

objectClass.name: Mode1
objectClass.semantics: Mode 1 data applicable to both IFF transponders and IFF interrogators.

attribute.name: IsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 1 is on.

attribute.name: IsDamaged
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 1 is damaged.

attribute.name: IsMalfunctioning
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 1 is malfunctioning.

objectClass.name: Mode1Interrogator
objectClass.semantics: IFF interrogator Mode 1 capability.

objectClass.name: Mode1Transponder
objectClass.semantics: IFF transponder Mode 1 capability.

attribute.name: Code
attribute.semantics: Mode 1 code. For Mark X/XII systems, two octal values in the range 00 to 73 with the first digit in the range 0 to 7 and the second digit in the range 0 to 3 reading left to right. The first (left) digit is Code Element 2 (A) and occupies bits 3 to 5, and the second (right) digit is Code Element 1 (B) and occupies bits 0 to 2. Mark XIIA systems may employ extended Mode 1 codes, four octal values in the range 0000 to 7777 (as per Mode 2 and Mode 3/A codes). The attribute ExtendedCodeIsSet indicates whether or not an extended code has been set. If an extended Mode 1 code has been set, Mark X/XII interrogators need to extract bits 9-11 (Code Element A) and bits 6-7 (Code Element B).

attribute.name: ExtendedCodeIsSet
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mark XIIA Enhanced Mode 1 is on.

objectClass.name: Mode2
objectClass.semantics: Mode 2 data applicable to both IFF transponders and IFF interrogators.

attribute.name: IsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 2 is on.

attribute.name: IsDamaged
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 2 is damaged.

attribute.name: IsMalfunctioning
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 2 is malfunctioning.

objectClass.name: Mode2Interrogator
objectClass.semantics: IFF interrogator Mode 2 capability.

objectClass.name: Mode2Transponder
objectClass.semantics: IFF transponder Mode 2 capability.

attribute.name: Code
attribute.semantics: Mode 2 code, four octal values in the range 0000 to 7777. The first (left-most) digit is Code Element 4 (A) and occupies bits 9 to 11, followed by Code Element 3 (B) in bits 6 to 8, Code Element 2 (C) in bits 3 to 5, and the last (right-most) digit is Code Element 1 (D) and occupies bits 0 to 2.

objectClass.name: Mode3A
objectClass.semantics: Mode 3/A data applicable to IFF/SSR transponders and IFF/SSR interrogators.

attribute.name: IsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 3/A is on.

attribute.name: IsDamaged
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 3/A is damaged.

attribute.name: IsMalfunctioning
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 3/A is malfunctioning.

objectClass.name: Mode3AInterrogator
objectClass.semantics: IFF/SSR interrogator Mode 3/A capability.

objectClass.name: Mode3ATransponder
objectClass.semantics: IFF/SSR transponder Mode 3/A capability.

attribute.name: Code
attribute.semantics: Mode 3/A code, four octal values in the range 0000 to 7777. The first (left-most) digit is Code Element 4 (A) and occupies bits 9 to 11, followed by Code Element 3 (B) in bits 6 to 8, Code Element 2 (C) in bits 3 to 5, and the last (right-most) digit is Code Element 1 (D) and occupies bits 0 to 2.

objectClass.name: Mode4
objectClass.semantics: Mode 4 data applicable to both IFF transponders and IFF interrogators.

attribute.name: IsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 4 is on.

attribute.name: IsDamaged
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 4 is damaged.

attribute.name: IsMalfunctioning
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 4 is malfunctioning.

attribute.name: PseudoCryptoCodeAAvailable
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether a Mode 4 pseudo crypto code (A) value is available in the attribute PseudoCryptoCodeA or that Challenge or Reply is to be used instead. If also a code B is available, the attribute PseudoCryptoCodeActive indicates which code is to be used.

attribute.name: PseudoCryptoCodeA
attribute.semantics: Mode 4 pseudo crypto code (A) in the range 0 to 4094 decimal.

attribute.name: PseudoCryptoCodeBAvailable
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether or not a Mode 4 pseudo crypto code B value is available in the attribute PseudoCryptoCodeB. If available, PseudoCryptoCodeA must also be set to a valid value, PseudoCryptoCodeAAvailable to True, and PseudoCryptoCodeActive to indicate which code is to be used. If not, the PseudoCryptoCodeA is to be used, if available, or the Challenge or Reply attribute.

attribute.name: PseudoCryptoCodeB
attribute.semantics: Mode 4 pseudo crypto code B in the range 0 to 4094 decimal.

attribute.name: PseudoCryptoCodeActive
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether Mode 4 Code A or Code B is active, or neither.

objectClass.name: Mode4Interrogator
objectClass.semantics: IFF interrogator Mode 4 capability.

attribute.name: Challenge
attribute.semantics: Indicates the validity of a Mode 4 interrogation. This attribute shall be set to Valid (1) or Invalid (2).

objectClass.name: Mode4Transponder
objectClass.semantics: IFF transponder Mode 4 capability.

attribute.name: Reply
attribute.semantics: Indicates the reply that would be received from a Mode 4 transponder by a Mode 4 interrogator. This attribute shall be set to Valid (1), Invalid (2), or No Response (3). The Unable to Verify (4) reply is optional.

objectClass.name: Mode5
objectClass.semantics: Mode 5 data applicable to both IFF transponders and IFF interrogators.

attribute.name: IsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 5 is on.

attribute.name: IsDamaged
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 5 is damaged.

attribute.name: IsMalfunctioning
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode 5 is malfunctioning.

attribute.name: PseudoCryptoCodeAvailable
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether a Mode 5 pseudo crypto code value is available in the attribute PseudoCryptoCode or that Reply is to be used instead.

attribute.name: PseudoCryptoCode
attribute.semantics: Mode 5 pseudo crypto code in the range 0 to 4094 decimal.

attribute.name: MessageFormatsCapable
attribute.semantics: The included message formats that this Mode 5 interrogator or transponder is capable of supporting and could be in a Mode 5 interrogation or in a reply to a Mode 5 interrogation.

attribute.name: Mission
attribute.semantics: The IFF mission of this Mode 5 interrogator or transponder. (Some of the enumerators are applicable to either a transponder or an interrogator.)

attribute.name: SpecificInformation
attribute.semantics: Specific information for Mode 5 data (DIS IFF PDU layer 3) that varies by System Type.

objectClass.name: Mode5Interrogator
objectClass.semantics: IFF interrogator Mode 5 capability.

objectClass.name: Mode5Transponder
objectClass.semantics: IFF transponder Mode 5 capability.

attribute.name: Reply
attribute.semantics: The validity of a reply that would be transmitted by a Mode 5 transponder if interrogated. The following enumerators shall be capable of being set: No Response (0), Valid (1), and Invalid (2). The Unable to Verify (3) value is optional.

attribute.name: LevelSelection
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether Mode 5 Level 1 (0) or Mode 5 Level 2 (1) is currently selected for this Mode 5 transponder. (The Level 2 selection includes both Level 1 and Level 2 message formats.)

attribute.name: SquitterIsOn
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether Mode 5 squitter is On (1) or Off (0).

attribute.name: Level2SquitterStatus
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether Mode 5 Level 2 squitter is Disabled (0) or Enabled (1).

attribute.name: PlatformType
attribute.semantics: Indicates the type of platform (air or ground) that is associated with this Mode 5 transponder.

attribute.name: PlatformIdentificationNumber
attribute.semantics: The Platform Identification Number (PIN) that uniquely identifies the platform carrying this Mode 5 transponder. The PIN shall be a number in the range 00 000 octal to 37 777 octal.

attribute.name: NationalOrigin
attribute.semantics: The national origin, the country that owns the platform carrying a Mode 5 transponder.

attribute.name: FigureOfMerit
attribute.semantics: The Figure of Merit, a number in the range of 0 to 31 decimal.

attribute.name: AntennaSelection
attribute.semantics: Indicate the present Mode 5 transponder antenna selection and shall be set to either No Statement (0) if the Mode 5 antenna position is not available or to Top (1), Bottom (2), or Diversity (3).

attribute.name: LineTestIsEnabled
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether a line test is in progress.

attribute.name: NavigationSource
attribute.semantics: The Navigation source.

attribute.name: LocationDataIsPresent
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether the source of Mode 5 latitude, longitude, and altitude information is present in the TransponderLocation attribute (1), or is to be computed locally (0).

attribute.name: TransponderLocation
attribute.semantics: Real-time location and altitude information transmitted by the transponder.

attribute.name: LocationErrorsDataPresence
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether there are location errors associated with Mode 5 latitude, longitude, or altitude present in the TransponderLocationError attribute (1), or No Location Errors are provided (0).

attribute.name: TransponderLocationError
attribute.semantics: The specification of errors in location and altitude information transmitted by the transponder.

objectClass.name: ModeC
objectClass.semantics: Mode C data applicable to both IFF/SSR transponders and IFF/SSR interrogators.

attribute.name: IsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode C is on.

attribute.name: IsDamaged
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode C is damaged.

attribute.name: IsMalfunctioning
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode C is malfunctioning.

objectClass.name: ModeCInterrogator
objectClass.semantics: IFF/SSR interrogator Mode C capability.

objectClass.name: ModeCTransponder
objectClass.semantics: IFF/SSR transponder Mode C capability.

attribute.name: AltitudeAvailable
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether the actual Mode C altitude is available in the attribute Altitude or is to be derived from entity truth data.

attribute.name: Altitude
attribute.semantics: The actual Mode C altitude.

objectClass.name: ModeS
objectClass.semantics: Mode S data applicable to both IFF/SSR transponders and IFF/SSR interrogators.

attribute.name: IsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode S is on.

attribute.name: IsDamaged
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode S is damaged.

attribute.name: IsMalfunctioning
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Mode S is malfunctioning.

attribute.name: LevelsPresent
attribute.semantics: Indicates the Mode S levels present. All levels that would be able to be responded to for a Mode S interrogation shall be set.

attribute.name: SpecificInformation
attribute.semantics: Specific information for Mode S data (DIS IFF PDU layer 4) that varies by System Type.

objectClass.name: ModeSInterrogator
objectClass.semantics: IFF/SSR interrogator Mode S capability.

attribute.name: InterrogatorIdentifier
attribute.semantics: Mode S Interrogator Code (IC) information.

attribute.name: TransmitState
attribute.semantics: The state of the Mode S interrogator. This field shall be set to No Statement (0) when the Mode S interrogator simulation is operating in the Regeneration Mode. It may be set to other values if operating in the Interactive Mode.

objectClass.name: ModeSTransponder
objectClass.semantics: IFF/SSR transponder Mode S capability.

attribute.name: AircraftAddress
attribute.semantics: A unique ICAO Mode S Aircraft Address. When a military aircraft has both a permanent and an operational Mode S Aircraft Address, only one address will be active at a time. The active aircraft address shall be included in this attribute.

attribute.name: AircraftIdentificationType
attribute.semantics: The aircraft identification type.

attribute.name: AircraftIdentification
attribute.semantics: The International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) flight identity of the aircraft, either the flight ID (i.e., call sign) or the registration number (also referred to as the tail number) of the aircraft. This attribute shall consist of an eight-element American Standard Code for Information Interchange (ASCII) character string consisting of the letters A to Z and the numbers 0 to 9. An aircraft identification may be less than eight characters. This string shall begin with the left-most character located at the lowest index. Unused characters shall be indicated by Null (0) values.

attribute.name: SquitterIsOn
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether the squitter is On (1) or Off (0).

attribute.name: SquitterType
attribute.semantics: Indicates the type of Mode S squitter.

attribute.name: CapabilityReport
attribute.semantics: The Mode S Transponder Capability Report. The numeric values are from Annex 10 to ICAO Vol. IV, para 3.1.2.5.2.2.1, except for No Statement. If no specific capability is indicated, this field shall be set to No Statement (255).

attribute.name: AircraftPresentDomain
attribute.semantics: Indicates the present domain of the aircraft. Surface means on a water platform surface such as a ship deck or on the water surface directly (e.g., pontoon-equipped aircraft). This field is referred to as Flight Status in ICAO Publications.

attribute.name: AltitudeAvailable
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether the actual Mode S altitude is available in the attribute Altitude or is to be derived from entity truth data.

attribute.name: Altitude
attribute.semantics: The actual Mode S altitude as determined by the Mode S transponder model, represented as a binary coded altitude referenced to -1000 feet. All altitudes greater than 50 175 feet shall be reported with 100-foot resolution. A receiving simulation is not required to process an actual Mode S altitude value by this standard and may always compute it from the entity truth altitude.

attribute.name: SquitterRecordSource
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether the squitter records are only present in the Squitter*Report attributes (0) or are also included in the GroundInitiatedCommBData array attribute (1).

attribute.name: AirbornePositionReportDataIsPresent
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether there is a Squitter Airborne Position Report IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the SquitterAirbornePositionReport attribute.

attribute.name: SquitterAirbornePositionReport
attribute.semantics: This replicates the functional data contained in a Squitter Airborne Position Report transmitted by a Mode S transponder in accordance with ICAO standards.

attribute.name: AirborneVelocityReportDataIsPresent
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether there is a Squitter Airborne Velocity Report IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the SquitterAirborneVelocityReport attribute.

attribute.name: SquitterAirborneVelocityReport
attribute.semantics: This replicates the functional data contained in a Squitter Airborne Velocity Report transmitted by a Mode S transponder in accordance with ICAO standards.

attribute.name: SurfacePositionReportDataIsPresent
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether there is a Squitter Surface Position Report IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the SquitterSurfacePositionReport attribute.

attribute.name: SquitterSurfacePositionReport
attribute.semantics: This replicates the functional data contained in a Squitter Surface Position Report transmitted by a Mode S transponder in accordance with ICAO standards. This report is transmitted when an aircraft is on the ground or aboard ship.

attribute.name: IdentificationReportDataIsPresent
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether there is a Squitter Identification Report IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the SquitterIdentificationReport attribute.

attribute.name: SquitterIdentificationReport
attribute.semantics: This replicates the functional data contained in a Squitter Identification Report transmitted by a Mode S transponder in accordance with ICAO standards.

attribute.name: EventDrivenReportDataIsPresent
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether there is a Squitter Event-Driven Report IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the SquitterEventDrivenReport attribute.

attribute.name: SquitterEventDrivenReport
attribute.semantics: This replicates the functional data contained in a Squitter Event-Driven Report transmitted by a Mode S transponder in accordance with ICAO standards.

attribute.name: TransponderLocation
attribute.semantics: Real-time location and altitude information transmitted by the transponder.

attribute.name: GroundInitiatedCommBData
attribute.semantics: Variable data related to Ground-Initiated Comm-B (GICB) data communications.

attribute.name: DAPSource
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether IFF Data records are present in the DAPData array attribute that can be used as the source for Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameters (DAPs).

attribute.name: DAPData
attribute.semantics: IFF Data records representing Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameters (DAPs).

attribute.name: LocationErrorsDataPresence
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether there are location errors associated with Mode S latitude, longitude, or altitude present in the TransponderLocationError attribute (1), or No Location Errors are provided (0).

attribute.name: TransponderLocationError
attribute.semantics: The specification of errors in location and altitude information transmitted by the transponder.

objectClass.name: TCASData
objectClass.semantics: TCAS/ACAS capability, applicable to IFF/SSR transponders, IFF/SSR interrogators, and TCAS/ACAS systems.

attribute.name: IsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not the TCAS/ACAS system is on.

attribute.name: IsDamaged
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not the TCAS/ACAS system is damaged.

attribute.name: IsMalfunctioning
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not the TCAS/ACAS system is malfunctioning.

attribute.name: TCAS_I_II_Type
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether a TCAS system is a TCAS I (0) or TCAS II (1) system when the TCAS/ACAS Type field does not indicate a TCAS III system. If the TCAS/ACAS Type field contains a nonzero value, then this field shall be set to No Statement (0).

attribute.name: BasicAdvancedIndicator
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether this TCACS/ACAS object contains Basic (0) or Advanced (1) data. Basic data is the minimum required to be set by any TCAS/ACAS capable interrogator or transponder. Advanced data means that the record contains additional data fields for a higher fidelity simulation of TCAS/ACAS capabilities. The attributes TCAS_ACASType, TCAS_ACASIndicator, and SoftwareVersion are not applicable to Basic and required for Advanced data.

attribute.name: TCAS_ACASType
attribute.semantics: Indicates the type of TCAS/ACAS system other than a TCAS I or II system, if known, or shall be set to No Statement (0) if not known.

attribute.name: TCAS_ACASIndicator
attribute.semantics: Indicates whether the record applies to a TCAS (0) or an ACAS (1) system.

attribute.name: SoftwareVersion
attribute.semantics: Indicates the software version of the TCAS/ACAS system if known or set to No Statement (0) if not known.

objectClass.name: Soviet
objectClass.semantics: Data applicable to both Soviet IFF transponders and Soviet IFF interrogators.

attribute.name: IsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not the Soviet mode is on.

attribute.name: IsDamaged
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not the Soviet mode is damaged.

attribute.name: IsMalfunctioning
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not Soviet mode is malfunctioning.

objectClass.name: SovietInterrogator
objectClass.semantics: IFF interrogator Soviet capability.

objectClass.name: SovietTransponder
objectClass.semantics: IFF transponder Soviet capability.

objectClass.name: RRB
objectClass.semantics: Reply Receiver 'B' (RRB) transponder capability.

attribute.name: IsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not the RRB transponder is set to reply.

attribute.name: IsDamaged
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not the RRB system is damaged.

attribute.name: IsMalfunctioning
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not the RRB system is malfunctioning.

attribute.name: Code
attribute.semantics: RRB Code (range 0-16).

attribute.name: PowerReductionIsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not power reduction is on.

attribute.name: RadarEnhancementIsOn
attribute.semantics: Specifies whether or not the radar enhancement is on.

interactionClass.name: IFFEmission
interactionClass.semantics: An IFF electromagnetic signal, used by IFF systems in Interactive Mode.

parameter.name: IFFSystemIdentifier
parameter.semantics: The identification of the IFFSystem object emitting this IFF electromagnetic signal or composite set of signals.

parameter.name: TransmissionIndicator
parameter.semantics: Indication whether this is an interrogation, a reply to an interrogation, or a squitter transmission.

parameter.name: InterrogatingObjectIdentifier
parameter.semantics: The object that is interrogating another object.

parameter.name: InterrogatedObjectIdentifier
parameter.semantics: The object to which the interrogation is being directed.

parameter.name: InteractiveEventIdentifier
parameter.semantics: Used to associate a reply to an interrogation. The Event Identifier is set by an interrogator that issues an interactive IFF emission, and the identical number is included in the reply by an interactive transponder to an interrogation. The first number assigned is one, and then is incremented by one for each succeeding interactive interrogation for the same IFF system. A transponder that has a capability to issue an interactive IFF transmission representing of one or more squitter message(s) shall always set the Event Identifier to zero.

parameter.name: ReportingSimulation
parameter.semantics: The simulation publishing this IFF emission.

parameter.name: DataCommunicationsSpecificInformation
parameter.semantics: Specific information for data communications (DIS IFF PDU layer 5) that varies by System Type.

parameter.name: BasicMode5
parameter.semantics: Basic data for an interactive Mode 5 interrogation or corresponding reply.

parameter.name: BasicModeS
parameter.semantics: Basic data for an interactive Mode S interrogation or corresponding reply.

parameter.name: Mode4Reply
parameter.semantics: Specification of a Mode 4 reply to an Interactive Mode interrogation.

parameter.name: Mode5Reply
parameter.semantics: Specification of a Mode 5 reply to an Interactive Mode interrogation.

parameter.name: ModeSDAPSource
parameter.semantics: Indicates whether IFF Data records are present in the ModeSDAPData array attribute that can be used as the source for Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameters (DAPs).

parameter.name: ModeSDAPData
parameter.semantics: IFF Data records representing Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameters (DAPs).

interactionClass.name: Squitter
interactionClass.semantics: An IFF transponder emission transmitting information without receiving an interrogation.

parameter.name: ModeSDAPSource
parameter.semantics: Indicates whether IFF Data records are present in the ModeSDAPData array attribute that can be used as the source for Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameters (DAPs).

parameter.name: ModeSDAPData
parameter.semantics: IFF Data records representing Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameters (DAPs).

simpleData.name: AngularAccelerationRadianPerSecondSquaredFloat32
simpleData.semantics: Angular acceleration composed of SI base units.

simpleData.name: Enumeration16
simpleData.semantics: Generic representation of a 16-bit enumeration. The enumerators for this enumeration are defined outside of the RPR FOM.

simpleData.name: Enumeration32
simpleData.semantics: Generic representation of a 32-bit enumeration. The enumerators for this enumeration are defined outside of the RPR FOM.

simpleData.name: Enumeration8
simpleData.semantics: Generic representation of an 8-bit enumeration. The enumerators for this enumeration are defined outside of the RPR FOM.

simpleData.name: PowerWattFloat32
simpleData.semantics: The unit of power is the watt (W), which is equal to one joule per second.

simpleData.name: PseudoCode12BitUnsignedInteger16
simpleData.semantics: Pseudo crypto code in the range 0 to 4094 decimal.

simpleData.name: RRBCodeUnsignedInteger8
simpleData.semantics: Decimal RRB code in (the least significant) 5 bits. See RRB documentation for the range of decimal values.

simpleData.name: TimeHundredthMicrosecond
simpleData.semantics: Time, based on SI base unit second, expressed in one-hundredths of a microsecond.

simpleData.name: TransponderAngle180Degree18Bit
simpleData.semantics: Two's complement coded angle in 18 bits (0-17) with the Most Significant Bit (MSB, 17) equal to -180 degrees.

simpleData.name: TransponderAngle90Degree18Bit
simpleData.semantics: Two's complement coded angle in 18 bits (0-17) with the Most Significant Bit (MSB, 17) equal to -90 degrees.

simpleData.name: TransponderAngleError180DegreeInteger32
simpleData.semantics: Delta angle represented as two's complement coded latitude with the Most Significant Bit (MSB) equal to -90 degrees.

simpleData.name: TransponderAngleError90DegreeInteger32
simpleData.semantics: Delta angle represented as two's complement coded latitude with the Most Significant Bit (MSB) equal to -90 degrees.

simpleData.name: TransponderCode4DigitUnsignedInteger16
simpleData.semantics: Four-digit octal code in the range 0000 to 7777 (base 8). The first (left-most) digit is Code Element 4 (A) and occupies bits 9 to 11, followed by Code Element 3 (B) in bits 6 to 8, Code Element 2 (C) in bits 3 to 5, and the last (right-most) digit is Code Element 1 (D) and occupies bits 0 to 2.

simpleData.name: TransponderDeltaAltitude100Foot
simpleData.semantics: Binary coded altitude in 11 bits (0-10) in 100-foot increments (range 0-100*(2^11)).

simpleData.name: TransponderDeltaAltitude25Foot
simpleData.semantics: Binary coded altitude in 11 bits (0-10) in 25-foot increments (range 0-25*(2^11)).

simpleData.name: TransponderModeCAltitude100Foot
simpleData.semantics: Binary coded Mode C altitude in 11 bits (0-10) in the range 0-126,000 feet in 100-foot increments.

simpleData.name: TransponderRelativeAltitude100Foot
simpleData.semantics: Binary coded altitude in 11 bits (0-10) in 100-foot increments referenced to -1000 feet.

simpleData.name: TransponderRelativeAltitude25Foot
simpleData.semantics: Binary coded altitude in 11 bits (0-10) in 25-foot increments referenced to -1000 feet.

simpleData.name: Undefined2Bit
simpleData.semantics: Undefined or undisclosed semantics of a 2-bit field.

simpleData.name: Undefined6Bit
simpleData.semantics: Undefined or undisclosed semantics of a 6-bit field.

enumeratedData.name: FundamentalParameterSystemSpecificDataTypeEnum32
enumeratedData.semantics: System-specific alternatives for fundamental energy radiation characteristics of a transponder or interrogator system emission.

arrayData.name: AntennaLocationIFFDataStructArray
arrayData.semantics: Array of Antenna Location IFF Data records.

arrayData.name: ASCIIcharArray8
arrayData.semantics: String of up to 8 ASCII characters. Unused characters shall be indicated by Null (0) values.

arrayData.name: FundamentalParameterDataStructArray
arrayData.semantics: Array of Fundamental Parameter Data records.

arrayData.name: GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStructArray
arrayData.semantics: Array of IFF Data records related to Mode S transponder Ground-Initiated Comm-B.

arrayData.name: ModeSDownlinkAircraftParameterVariantStructArray
arrayData.semantics: Array of IFF Data records related to Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameters.

fixedRecordData.name: AngleDeceptionAngleDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Data defining the angle deception jamming in one plane.

field.name: Offset
field.semantics: Indicates the relative angle at which the deceptive radar returns are centered. This angle is measured with respect to the x-y plane of the victim radar's entity coordinate system. This angle is measured in radians from the victim radar entity's azimuth/elevation for the true jammer position to the center of the range of azimuths/elevations in which deceptive radar returns are perceived. For azimuth offset, positive and negative values indicate that the perceived positions of the jammer are right and left of the true position of the jammer, respectively. The range of permissible values is -pi to pi. For elevation offset, positive and negative values indicate that the perceived positions of the jammer are above and below the true position of the jammer, respectively. The range of permissible values is -pi/2 to pi/2.

field.name: Width
field.semantics: Indicates the range through which the deceptive radar returns are perceived, centered on the Offset. For azimuth width, the range of permissible values is 0 to 2pi radians. For elevation width, the range of permissible values is 0 to pi radians.

field.name: PullRate
field.semantics: Indicates the rate (in radians per second) at which the Offset value is changing. For azimuth pull rate, positive and negative values indicate that the azimuth offset is moving to the right or left, respectively. For elevation pull rate, positive and negative values indicate that the elevation offset is moving up or down, respectively.

field.name: PullAcceleration
field.semantics: Indicates the rate (in radians per second squared) at which the PullRate value is changing. Azimuth Pull Acceleration is defined as positive to the right and negative to the left. Elevation Pull Acceleration is defined as positive to upward and negative downward.

fixedRecordData.name: AngleDeceptionStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Specification of discrete values associated with angle deception jamming that cannot be referenced to an emitter mode. The values provided in the record shall be considered valid only for the victim radar beams listed in the jamming beam's JammedObjectIdentifiers. See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 6.2.21.2 for more information, including Figure 46 and Figure 47 illustrating the concept of angle deception jamming.

field.name: AzimuthAngleData
field.semantics: The azimuth relative angle, width, pull rate, and pull acceleration.

field.name: ElevationAngleData
field.semantics: The elevation relative angle, width, pull rate, and pull acceleration.

fixedRecordData.name: AntennaLocationIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Specification of the location of an auxiliary IFF antenna for a transponder or interrogator.

field.name: SystemName
field.semantics: The name of the system with which this auxiliary IFF antenna is associated.

field.name: AntennaReferenceNumber
field.semantics: The reference number associated with each auxiliary antenna.

field.name: AntennaStatus
field.semantics: The status of the auxiliary antenna.

field.name: RelativeAntennaLocation
field.semantics: The location of the auxiliary antenna relative to the associated emitting entity's coordinate system.

fixedRecordData.name: BlankingSectorStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Specification of a persistent area within a scan volume where emitter power is reduced to an insignificant value. See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 6.2.21.1 for more information, including Figure 45 illustrating an example.

field.name: LeftAzimuth
field.semantics: Indicates the left-most azimuth (clockwise in radians) for which emitted power is reduced. This angle is measured in the x-y plane of the radar's entity coordinate system. The range of permissible values is 0 to 2pi, with zero pointing in the x-direction.

field.name: RightAzimuth
field.semantics: Indicates the right-most azimuth (clockwise in radians) for which emitted power is reduced. This angle is measured in the x-y plane of the radar's entity coordinate system. The range of permissible values is 0 to 2pi, with zero pointing in the x-direction.

field.name: LowerElevation
field.semantics: Indicates the lowest elevation (in radians) for which emitted power is reduced. This angle is measured positive upward with respect to the x-y plane of the radar's entity coordinate system. The range of permissible values is -pi/2 to pi/2.

field.name: UpperElevation
field.semantics: Indicates the highest elevation (in radians) for which emitted power is reduced. This angle is measured positive upward with respect to the x-y plane of the radar's entity coordinate system. The range of permissible values is -pi/2 to pi/2.

field.name: ResidualPower
field.semantics: Specifies the residual effective radiated power in the blanking sector in dBm.

fixedRecordData.name: DeltaBarometricAltitudeStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: The delta error in barometric altitude of the entity.

field.name: DeltaAltitude
field.semantics: The delta barometric altitude.

field.name: PositiveNegativeIndicator
field.semantics: Indicates whether the delta altitude value is Positive (0) or Negative (1). The Positive indicator means the offset is above the entity's present truth altitude and a Negative indicator means the offset is below the entity's present truth altitude.

fixedRecordData.name: DeltaMode5SAltitudeStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: The delta error in Mode 5 / Mode S Altitude of the entity with a Mode 5 or S transponder.

field.name: DeltaAltitude
field.semantics: The delta Mode 5 / Mode S altitude.

field.name: PositiveNegativeIndicator
field.semantics: Indicates whether the delta altitude value is Positive (0) or Negative (1). The Positive indicator means the offset is above the entity's present truth altitude and a Negative indicator means the offset is below the entity's present truth altitude.

fixedRecordData.name: FalseTargetsStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Specification of discrete values that are associated with false targets jamming that cannot be referenced to an emitter mode. The values provided in the record shall be considered valid only for the victim radar beams listed in the jamming beam's JammedObjectIdentifiers. See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 6.2.21.3 for more information, including Figure 48 illustrating the effect.

field.name: FalseTargetCount
field.semantics: Specifies the number of false targets presented to the victim radar.

field.name: WalkSpeed
field.semantics: Specifies the speed (in meters per second) at which false targets move toward the victim radar. Negative values shall indicate a velocity away from the victim radar.

field.name: WalkAcceleration
field.semantics: Specifies the rate (in meters per second squared) at which false targets accelerate toward the victim radar. Negative values shall indicate an acceleration direction away from the victim radar.

field.name: MaximumWalkDistance
field.semantics: Specifies the distance (in meters) that a false target is to walk before it pauses in range.

field.name: KeepTime
field.semantics: Specifies the simulation time (in seconds) that a false target is to be held at the MaximumWalkDistance before it resets to its initial position.

field.name: EchoSpacing
field.semantics: Specifies the distance between false targets in meters. A positive value for this field shall indicate that the second and subsequent false targets appear at the given spacing relative to the first false target away from the victim radar (i.e., at increasing ranges from the victim). A negative value shall indicate that the second and subsequent false targets appear toward the victim radar relative to the first false target.

field.name: FirstTargetOffset
field.semantics: Sets the position of the first false target relative to the jamming entity in meters. A positive value for this field shall indicate that the first target appears at the given distance relative to the jamming entity away from the victim radar (i.e., at an increasing range from the victim). A negative value shall indicate the first false target appears toward the victim radar relative to the jamming entity. A value of zero shall place the first false target at the same position as the jamming entity.

fixedRecordData.name: FundamentalParameterDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Fundamental Parameter Data record.

field.name: EIRP
field.semantics: The average peak radiated power of the emission.

field.name: Frequency
field.semantics: The center frequency of the emission.

field.name: PgRF
field.semantics: When applied to originators, this field shall specify the number of interrogations per second emitted. This field shall be set to zero when applied to a responder (i.e. transponder) systems.

field.name: PulseWidth
field.semantics: The duration of the fundamental pulse of which the interrogation or reply is composed.

field.name: BurstLength
field.semantics: The number of emissions generated in a single burst. This field shall contain zero for continuously emitting systems and shall contain the value 1 for responders.

field.name: ApplicableModes
field.semantics: Specifies the modes to which the fundamental parameter data applies.

field.name: SystemSpecificData
field.semantics: System specific data associated with the system type.

fixedRecordData.name: GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Information contained in a Transponder/Interrogator (T/I) data link message in Ground-Initiated Comm-B (GICB) format.

field.name: TILinkType
field.semantics: The T/I link type.

field.name: MessageDesignator
field.semantics: The GICB message designator as defined in appropriate ICAO technical publications, represented by a 16-bit enumeration.

field.name: RegisterMessageNumber
field.semantics: The applicable register and message number for this message.

field.name: ProcessStatus
field.semantics: GICB Process Status.

field.name: RegisterDataValidity
field.semantics: GICB Register Data Validity.

field.name: Register
field.semantics: GICB register data.

field.name: Message
field.semantics: 56-bit GICB message data.

fixedRecordData.name: InteractiveBasicMode5IFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Basic data for an interactive Mode 5 interrogation or corresponding reply.

field.name: MessageFormats
field.semantics: For a Mode 5 interrogator, this field contains the specific Message Formats being requested. These message formats may be different from those contained in the regeneration IFF object for this interrogator. For a Mode 5 transponder, this field contains the specific Message Formats that it actually responded to for this interrogation. This may be different from the Message Formats contained in the interrogation request, as well as the present Message Formats contained in the regeneration IFF object for this transponder.

fixedRecordData.name: InteractiveBasicModeSIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Basic data for an interactive Mode S interrogation or corresponding reply.

field.name: Levels
field.semantics: For a Mode S interrogator, this field indicates the Mode S levels it is interrogating. For a Mode S transponder, this field indicates the actual levels contained in this reply. The value of this field may or may not be the same as the present value for the ModeSLevelsPresent attribute of the IFF object.

field.name: TransmitState
field.semantics: The Mode S Transmit State that would be contained in this specific Mode S interactive interrogation. The value of this field may or may not be the same as the present value for the ModeSTransmitState attribute of the IFF object. Not applicable to a transponder and set to zero.

field.name: InterrogatorIdentifier
field.semantics: The Mode S Interrogator Identifier that would be contained in this specific Mode S interactive interrogation. The values of this record may or may not be the same as the present value for the ModeSInterrogatorIdentifier attribute of the IFF object. Not applicable to a transponder and set to zero.

fixedRecordData.name: InteractiveMode4ReplyIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Specification of an interactive Mode 4 reply to an Interactive Mode 4 interrogation.

field.name: Reply
field.semantics: The Mode 4 reply that would be sent by this transponder to this Interactive Mode 4 interrogation. This may not be the same as the regeneration value.

fixedRecordData.name: InteractiveMode5ReplyIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: The specification of an Interactive Mode 5 reply to an Interactive Mode 5 interrogation.

field.name: Reply
field.semantics: The Mode 5 reply that would be sent by this IFF system to this Interactive Mode 5 interrogation.

fixedRecordData.name: InterrogatedModesStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: The specific modes and other basic data types related to an interactive interrogation, reply, or squitter transmission.

field.name: Mode1Included
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode 1 is being interrogated or contained in a transponder reply.

field.name: Mode2Included
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode 2 is being interrogated or contained in a transponder reply.

field.name: Mode3AIncluded
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode 3/A is being interrogated or contained in a transponder reply.

field.name: Mode4Included
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode 4 is being interrogated or contained in a transponder reply.

field.name: Mode5Included
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode 5 is being interrogated or contained in a transponder reply.

field.name: ModeSIncluded
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode S is being interrogated or contained in a transponder reply.

field.name: ModeCIncluded
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode C Altitude is being interrogated or contained in a transponder reply.

field.name: TCASIncluded
field.semantics: Set to True when TCAS/ACAS is being interrogated or contained in a transponder reply.

field.name: SpecialIPIncluded
field.semantics: Set to True when this is a Special Identification of Position (IP) transmission.

field.name: SquitterIncluded
field.semantics: Set to True when this is an interactive squitter transmission.

fixedRecordData.name: InterrogatorSideLobeSuppressionStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Interrogator Side Lobe Suppression (ISLS) inhibits transponder replies to all challenges not radiated in the main beam of the interrogating antenna.

field.name: AntennaType
field.semantics: The type of antenna used by the system.

field.name: PulseRiseTime
field.semantics: The real-world time required for the leading edge of pulses P1, P2, and P3 to rise from 10 to 90 percent of its maximum voltage amplitude. Pulse Rise Time shall be expressed in one-hundredths of a microsecond where 1 is 0.01 microsecond and 99 is 0.99 microsecond.

field.name: PulseFallTime
field.semantics: The real-world time required for the leading edge of pulses P1, P2, and P3 to fall from 10 to 90 percent of its maximum voltage amplitude. Pulse Fall Time shall be expressed in one-hundredths of a microsecond where 1 is 0.01 microsecond and 99 is 0.99 microsecond.

fixedRecordData.name: JammingTechniqueStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Record specifying the kind, category, subcategory, and specific indication of a jamming technique.

field.name: Kind
field.semantics: Indicates the broadest category of jamming.

field.name: Category
field.semantics: Permits more detailed definition of techniques defined by the Kind.

field.name: Subcategory
field.semantics: Permits more detailed definition of techniques defined by the Kind and Category.

field.name: Specific
field.semantics: Provides a means for high-fidelity electronic warfare emitters to indicate the most detailed definition of techniques.

fixedRecordData.name: Mode5MessageFormatsStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: The message formats associated with an IFF Mode 5 transponder or interrogator. When used for a transponder object in the Regeneration mode, it indicates the message formats that the transponder is capable of supporting and could be in a reply to a Mode 5 interrogation. When used for a transponder in the Interactive mode, in a Reply or Squitter interaction, it indicates the specific message formats that it actually responded to for the Mode 5 interrogation. When used for an interrogator object in the Regeneration mode, it indicates the message formats that the interrogator is capable of supporting and could be sent in a Mode 5 interrogation. When used for an interrogator in the Interactive mode, in an Interrogation interaction, it indicates the specific Mode 5 message formats being requested.

field.name: MessageFormat0Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 0 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat1Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 1 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat2Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 2 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat3Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 3 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat4Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 4 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat5Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 5 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat6Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 6 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat7Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 7 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat8Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 8 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat9Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 9 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat10Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 10 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat11Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 11 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat12Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 12 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat13Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 13 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat14Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 14 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat15Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 15 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat16Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 16 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat17Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 17 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat18Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 18 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat19Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 19 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat20Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 20 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat21Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 21 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat22Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 22 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat23Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 23 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat24Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 24 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat25Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 25 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat26Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 26 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat27Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 27 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat28Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 28 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat29Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 29 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat30Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 30 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

field.name: MessageFormat31Included
field.semantics: Set to True when a message format 31 capability exists or is included in an interactive interrogation or reply.

fixedRecordData.name: Mode5STransponderLocationErrorIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: The specification of errors in location and altitude information transmitted by a Mode 5 or Mode S transponder. Errors are defined as delta values from the truth location of the entity platform. They reflect either human or navigation system errors that might be present and manifest themselves in the data sent in a Mode 5 or Mode S reply or squitter report. If Mode 5 or S location errors are modeled, then this record shall be issued to convey the magnitude of the error for one or more of the location values that could have an error. Fields with no errors shall have all bits of that field set to zero.

field.name: DeltaLatitude
field.semantics: The delta error in latitude from the true latitude computed for the entity with a Mode 5 or S transponder. Delta latitude shall be represented as two's complement coded latitude with the Most Significant Bit (MSB) equal to -90 degrees. All bits of this field shall be set to zero if there is no error in latitude.

field.name: DeltaLongitude
field.semantics: The delta error in longitude from the true longitude computed for the entity. Delta longitude shall be represented as two's complement coded longitude with the Most Significant Bit (MSB) equal to -180 degrees. All bits of this field shall be set to zero if there is no error in longitude.

field.name: DeltaMode5SAltitude
field.semantics: The delta error in Mode 5 / Mode S Altitude of the entity with a Mode 5 or S transponder. All bits of this field shall be set to zero if there is no error in Mode 5 / Mode S altitude.

field.name: DeltaBarometricAltitude
field.semantics: The delta error in barometric altitude of the entity. All bits of this field shall be set to zero if there is no error in barometric altitude.

fixedRecordData.name: Mode5STransponderLocationIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Real-time location and altitude information transmitted by a Mode 5 or Mode S transponder.

field.name: Latitude
field.semantics: The latitude of the entity, represented as an 18-bit two's complement coded angle with the Most Significant Bit (MSB) equal to -90 degrees.

field.name: Longitude
field.semantics: The longitude of the entity, represented as an 18-bit two's complement coded angle with the Most Significant Bit (MSB) equal to -180 degrees.

field.name: Mode5SAltitude
field.semantics: The Mode 5 / Mode S Altitude of the entity.

field.name: BarometricAltitude
field.semantics: The Barometric Altitude of the entity.

fixedRecordData.name: ModeCAltitudeStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: The actual Mode C altitude.

field.name: PositiveNegativeIndicator
field.semantics: Indicates either a Positive Altitude Above MSL (0) or a Negative Altitude Below MSL (1).

field.name: ModeCAltitude
field.semantics: The actual Mode C altitude, in the range 0 feet to 126 000 feet in 100-foot increments based on the PositiveNegativeIndicator field. A value of 2047 indicates to use Alternate Mode C Altitude (i.e., derived from entity truth data).

fixedRecordData.name: ModeSDAPSourceStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Mode S source of Downlink Aircraft Parameters (DAPs).

field.name: IndicatedAirSpeedDataIsPresent
field.semantics: Indicates whether there is an IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the IFFData array attribute that can be used as the source for the Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameter (DAP) for the Indicated Air Speed (IAS).

field.name: MachNumberDataIsPresent
field.semantics: Indicates whether there is an IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the IFFData array attribute that can be used as the source for the Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameter (DAP) for the Mach Number.

field.name: GroundSpeedDataIsPresent
field.semantics: Indicates whether there is an IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the IFFData array attribute that can be used as the source for the Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameter (DAP) for the Ground Speed.

field.name: MagneticHeadingDataIsPresent
field.semantics: Indicates whether there is an IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the IFFData array attribute that can be used as the source for the Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameter (DAP) for the Magnetic Heading.

field.name: TrackAngleRateDataIsPresent
field.semantics: Indicates whether there is an IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the IFFData array attribute that can be used as the source for the Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameter (DAP) for the Track Angle Rate.

field.name: TrueTrackAngleDataIsPresent
field.semantics: Indicates whether there is an IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the IFFData array attribute that can be used as the source for the Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameter (DAP) for the True Track Angle.

field.name: TrueAirspeedDataIsPresent
field.semantics: Indicates whether there is an IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the IFFData array attribute that can be used as the source for the Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameter (DAP) for the True Airspeed.

field.name: VerticalRateDataIsPresent
field.semantics: Indicates whether there is an IFF Data record either Present (1) or Not Present (0) in the IFFData array attribute that can be used as the source for the Mode S Downlink Aircraft Parameter (DAP) for the Vertical Rate.

fixedRecordData.name: ModeSInterrogatorIdentifierStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: Mode S Interrogator Code (IC) information.

field.name: PrimaryICCode
field.semantics: Primary IC Code.

field.name: SecondaryICCode
field.semantics: Secondary IC Code.

fixedRecordData.name: ModeSLevelsPresentStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: The Mode S levels the transponder or interrogator is able to support.

field.name: Level1IsPresent
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode S level 1 is present.

field.name: Level2ElementarySurveillanceIsPresent
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode S level 2 (Elementary Surveillance sublevel) is present.

field.name: Level2EnhancedSurveillanceIsPresent
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode S level 2 (Enhanced Surveillance sublevel) is present.

field.name: Level3IsPresent
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode S level 3 is present.

field.name: Level4IsPresent
field.semantics: Set to True when Mode S level 4 is present.

fixedRecordData.name: SquitterAirbornePositionReportIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: This replicates the functional data contained in a Squitter Airborne Position Report transmitted by a Mode S transponder in accordance with ICAO standards. If it is desired to transmit an exact replication of this squitter report, it may be sent using the GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStruct.

field.name: AircraftAddressSource
field.semantics: The source of the Aircraft Address.

field.name: FormatType
field.semantics: The Format Type.

field.name: ICAOSpare
field.semantics: Represent the spare field that exists in a Squitter Airborne Position Report.

field.name: SurveillanceStatus
field.semantics: Indicates the surveillance status associated with this report.

field.name: TurnRateSource
field.semantics: Indicates whether the aircraft is turning or whether the receiving simulation is to make that determination by examining entity truth data.

field.name: AltitudeSource
field.semantics: Indicates whether the altitude is to be computed locally or is provided in a Mode5STransponderLocationIFFDataStruct.

field.name: TimeTypeSource
field.semantics: Indicates whether this squitter report was output on an even or odd second or whether the receiving simulation is to determine that based on its Mode S interrogator model.

field.name: LatLongSource
field.semantics: Indicates whether the lat/long is to be computed locally or is provided in a Mode5STransponderLocationIFFDataStruct.

fixedRecordData.name: SquitterAirborneVelocityReportIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: This replicates the functional data contained in a Squitter Airborne Velocity Report transmitted by a Mode S transponder in accordance with ICAO standards. If it is desired to transmit an exact replication of this squitter report, it may be sent using the GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStruct.

field.name: AircraftAddressSource
field.semantics: The source of the Aircraft Address.

field.name: FormatType
field.semantics: The Format Type.

field.name: Subtype
field.semantics: The subtype associated with this report. (See the appropriate ICAO publication for the subtype values.)

field.name: IsSynced
field.semantics: Indicates the sync status as being either Synced (True) or Not Synced (False).

field.name: GroundTrackSource
field.semantics: Indicates whether the ground track is to be computed locally from truth data or is provided in a GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStruct.

field.name: TRNRateSource
field.semantics: Indicates whether the Turn (TRN) Rate is to be computed locally from truth data or is provided in a GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStruct.

field.name: VRTRateSource
field.semantics: Indicates whether the Vertical Rate of Climb (VRT) Rate is to be computed locally from truth data or is provided in a GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStruct.

fixedRecordData.name: SquitterEventDrivenReportIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: This replicates the functional data contained in a Squitter Event-Driven Report transmitted by a Mode S transponder in accordance with ICAO standards. If it is desired to transmit an exact replication of this squitter report, it may be sent using the GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStruct.

field.name: SquitterEventIdentification
field.semantics: The ICAO Downlink Format (DF) number assigned to this type of event data.

field.name: SquitterEventData
field.semantics: The specific event data being sent.

fixedRecordData.name: SquitterIdentificationReportIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: This replicates the functional data contained in a Squitter Identification Report transmitted by a Mode S transponder in accordance with ICAO standards. If it is desired to transmit an exact replication of this squitter report, it may be sent using the GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStruct.

field.name: AircraftAddressSource
field.semantics: The source of the Aircraft Address.

field.name: FormatType
field.semantics: The Format Type.

field.name: AircraftTypeWake
field.semantics: Aircraft type and wake data.

field.name: AircraftIDSource
field.semantics: The data source for the Aircraft ID.

field.name: ICAOSpare
field.semantics: Represent the spare field that exists in a Squitter Identification Report.

fixedRecordData.name: SquitterSurfacePositionReportIFFDataStruct
fixedRecordData.semantics: This replicates the functional data contained in a Squitter Surface Position Report transmitted by a Mode S transponder in accordance with ICAO standards. This report is transmitted when an aircraft is on the ground or aboard ship. If it is desired to transmit an exact replication of this squitter report, it may be sent using the GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStruct.

field.name: AircraftAddressSource
field.semantics: The source of the Aircraft Address.

field.name: FormatType
field.semantics: The Format Type.

field.name: Movement
field.semantics: Movement data.

field.name: HeadingSource
field.semantics: Indicates whether the heading is to be computed locally from truth data or is provided in a GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStruct.

field.name: VRTRateSource
field.semantics: Indicates whether the Vertical Rate of Climb (VRT) Rate is to be computed locally from truth data or is provided in a GroundInitiatedCommBIFFDataStruct.

field.name: ICAOSpare
field.semantics: Represents the 24-bit ICAO Spare field contained in a Squitter Surface Position Report.

variantRecordData.name: FundamentalParameterSystemSpecificVariantStruct

alternative.name: None
alternative.semantics: No fundamental energy radiation characteristics of a transponder or interrogator system. See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 6.2.44.

alternative.name: InterrogatorSideLobeSuppression
alternative.semantics: Interrogator Side Lobe Suppression (ISLS) inhibits transponder replies to all challenges not radiated in the main beam of the interrogating antenna.

variantRecordData.semantics: System-specific alternatives for fundamental energy radiation characteristics of a transponder or interrogator system emission.

variantRecordData.name: ModeSDownlinkAircraftParameterVariantStruct
variantRecordData.semantics: For optional Mode S transponder Downlink Aircraft Parameter data.

variantRecordData.name: TransponderDeltaAltitudeVariantStruct
variantRecordData.semantics: Delta to the transponder reported altitude represented as a 11-bit binary coded altitude.

variantRecordData.name: TransponderRelativeAltitudeVariantStruct
variantRecordData.semantics: Transponder reported altitude represented as a 11-bit binary coded altitude referenced to -1000 feet. All altitudes greater than 50175 feet shall be reported with 100-foot resolution. When no altitude is reported, the alternative value shall be set to 2047 (decimal) to indicate to use entity truth altitude to derive the transponder reported altitude.

note.label: RPRnoteDER1
note.semantics: If there is no object instance associated with the attribute, then this should be set to the empty string (no characters). Refer to SISO-STD-001 Section 7.8.6 for handling empty strings.

note.label: RPRnoteDER2
note.semantics:
The update condition shall be evaluated as follows: The owner of a designator object shall maintain two state models of the object in support of the dead reckoning process. One model shall be the internal model used by the simulation application to represent that object. The other shall be a dead reckoning model of the object. Certain thresholds shall be established as criteria for determining if the object's actual spot location has varied by an allowable amount from the dead reckoned spot location. The attribute is updated when the object's actual spot location differs from the dead reckoned spot location by more than DRA_POS_THRSH (default value: 1 m). See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Sections 4.6.6 and 5.3.2.3 and Annex E for more details.
A TSPI update includes all attributes applicable to the dead reckoning algorithm. A TSPI update attribute may be omitted if its last updated value is within an established epsilon amount from its current value.

note.label: RPRnoteDER3
note.semantics: The epsilon value for the location is DRA_POS_EPSILSON (default value: 0.001 m), in any direction.

note.label: RPRnoteDER4
note.semantics: The epsilon value for the linear acceleration is DRA_ACCEL_EPSILSON (default value: 0.001 m/(s^2)), in any direction.

note.label: RPRnoteDER5
note.semantics: The EventIdentifier attribute is used to link together updates arising from a common event. For example an emitter function change can be linked to a frequency change in one of its associated emitter beams, by using the same event ID in the attribute updates to the appropriate EmitterSystem and EmitterBeam classes. It is important to note that in order that the EventIdentifier is delivered in the same reflect attribute values call in the receiving federates as the associated attributes, then all the attributes, including the EventIdentifier, must share the same transport type.

note.label: RPRnoteDER6
note.semantics: The federate should change the value of the EventIdentifier when associating changes between two or more emitter beams and/or emitter systems. It is not necessary to update the EventIdentifier field with every attribute update if the change is not associated with changes in other instances of other classes.

note.label: RPRnoteDER7
note.semantics: If emissions data is available (determined by the state of the EmissionsDataAvailable attribute) then this attribute shall be updated by the federate. If emissions data is not available then this attribute shall not be updated by the federate.

note.label: RPRnoteDER8
note.semantics: This attribute is updated if the current value differs from the previously updated value by more than the value specified by the symbolic name (default value: 3 degrees).

note.label: RPRnoteDER9
note.semantics: This attribute is updated if the current value differs from the previously updated value by more than the value specified by the symbolic name (default value: 3 degrees).

note.label: RPRnoteDER10
note.semantics: A change in sweep sync value alone does not require an update to be published. When one of the other beam attributes (center and sweep for azimuth and elevation ) requires an update publication, the sweep sync attribute shall be included when its value has changed since the last update.

note.label: RPRnoteDER11
note.semantics: This attribute is updated if the current value differs from the previously updated value by more than the value specified by the symbolic name (default value: 1 degree).

note.label: RPRnoteDER12
note.semantics: This attribute is updated if the current value differs from the previously updated value by more than the value specified by the symbolic name (default value: 1 degree).

note.label: RPRnoteDER13
note.semantics: The term "ERP" is widely used throughout the radar community (including radar databases), but in radar contexts, the intended meaning is actually that of EIRP.

note.label: RPRnoteDER14
note.semantics: This must reference a valid Object instance.

note.label: RPRnoteDER15
note.semantics: The object IDs in this structure shall refer to PhysicalEntity objects (or subclasses of PhysicalEntity).

note.label: RPRnoteDER16
note.semantics: Special update conditions may apply to the datatypes or their fields used for the array elements. Refer to the datatype or field semantics, including any referenced notes, for more details.

note.label: RPRnoteDER17
note.semantics: The object IDs in this structure shall refer to EmitterBeam objects (or subclasses of EmitterBeam).

note.label: RPRnoteDER18
note.semantics: If the IFF system is Mode 5 capable then this attribute shall be updated by the federate. If the IFF system is not Mode 5 capable then this attribute shall either not be published or set to False.

note.label: RPRnoteDER19
note.semantics: The RRB system is a transponder only system.

note.label: RPRnoteDER20
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. For azimuth related data, the threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_AZ_THRSH (default value: 1 degree). For elevation related data, the threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_EL_THRSH (default value: 1 degree).

note.label: RPRnoteDER21
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_AD_PULRAT_THRSH (default value: 0.017 rad/s).

note.label: RPRnoteDER22
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_AD_PULACC_THRSH (default value: 0.017 rad/(s^2)).

note.label: RPRnoteDER23
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_FT_VEL_THRSH (default value: 1.0 m/s).

note.label: RPRnoteDER24
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_FT_ACC_THRSH (default value: 1.0 m/(s^2)).

note.label: RPRnoteDER25
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_FT_MWD_THRSH (default value: 10000 m).

note.label: RPRnoteDER26
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_FT_KT_THRSH (default value: 10 s).

note.label: RPRnoteDER27
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_FT_ESP_THRSH (default value: 10 m).

note.label: RPRnoteDER28
note.semantics: For radar-like IFF interrogators, this may still be known as 'ERP' because EIRP is generally known throughout the radar community as ERP. The field is named "ERP" in IEEE Std 1278.1-2012, but it is believed that EIRP was the intended meaning.

note.label: RPRnoteDER29
note.semantics: Frequency modulation shall be derived from database parameters stored for the particular emitting system and specified mode.

note.label: RPRnoteDER30
note.semantics: IFF Data records have not been defined for specific DAP data. When such records are developed, it is recommended that the DAP field format from the appropriate ICAO publication be used.

note.label: RPRnoteDER31
note.semantics: The size of this field in IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section B.2.51 is assumed to be incorrect. According to ICAO documentation, this field in the extended squitter surface position BDS register is 7 bits, not 3. RPR FOM users should be careful in using the full range as per the ICAO specifications. For in heterogeneous exercises with DIS-based simulations this may lead to unexpected results. It is advised to explicitly note the allowed values and the mapping to be applied in gateways in federation/exercise agreements (when using Mode S squitter surface position reports).

note.label: RPRnoteDER32
note.semantics: At the time of publication of the Standard for RPR FOM 3.0, no IFF Data records have been defined for specific DAP data.
